lyrics:
Headed down south to the land of the pines
Thumbing my way into North Caroline
Staring up the road and pray to God I see headlights
I made it down the coast in seventeen hours
Picking me a bouquet of dogwood flowers

You're as smooth as Tennessee whiskey
You're as sweet as strawberry wine
You're as warm as a glass of brandy
And honey, I stay stoned on your love all the time

Had a little fun, then we tried to grow up
Found a little spot, had a little pickup truck
We were young and wild, we were free
Then the rain came down and washed it all away
